Based on the retrieved material primarily focused around systemic therapy for prostate cancer, none of them seem relevant to determining liability for hiding critical information following an autopsy. Since we had already deduced that Section 193 of the IPC would apply to the scenario presented due to preventing investigation purposes, my stance remains unchanged. Concealing crucial details after conducting an autopsy falls under obstructionist behavior falling under IPC 193 which prohibits intentionally hindering justice.
